channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Executive Viewpoint: How AI Is Changing the Role of the Insurance Broker
item.description: AI is redefining the role of the insurance broker, as it moves the function away from transactional activity and toward more strategic, client-led advice built on better use of data. Discussion in the insurance industry has often centered on whether AI will replace people, but the more pertinent question is how it is changing broking and underwriting roles. AI is not replacing the broker, but it is changing how brokers compete.

item.title: Aon ups data centre lifecycle insurance program capacity to US$3.5bn
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Tokio Marine Management Australasia names new Japan business department head
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: FCA takes next steps toward enforcement action against Hartley Pensions and an individual
item.description: The FCA has set out plans to take action against Hartley Pensions Limited and an individual involved at the firm. The FCA alleges that Hartley provided it with false and misleading information and improperly withdrew and invested substantial amounts of customers’ pension funds, without their consent, to benefit an individual at the firm. Hartley was a Self-Invested Personal Pension operator, which went into administration in July 2022.

item.title: I Sold My Startup A Year After Founding It. Here’s Why That Was The Fastest Way To Build Real-World Healthcare AI
item.description: Less than a year later, we faced a critical fork in the road: raise venture capital and continue independently, or accept an acquisition offer from Radiology Partners , the world’s largest radiology practice. We decided that joining forces — carefully structured to protect our velocity — would dramatically improve the odds that we realize our mission of significantly increasing the world’s access to healthcare. On top of this, real-world radiology is defined by edge cases where rare but critical pathologies are encountered regularly.

item.title: YC Once Again Tops Ranks Of Most Active Fintech Investors In Q1 Even As Deal Count Drops
item.description: That was the overall trend for fintech startup funding in Q1, and it held when looking at the rankings of the most active investors in the space, with even frontrunner Y Combinator participating in fewer deals in the sector last quarter. YC also topped the list of the most active fintech investors in rounds of $5 million or above, participating in 14 such transactions. Lightspeed and Coinbase Ventures came in next on the list of most active investors in rounds of $5 million or more — each writing checks into nine fintech startup investments during the 2026 first quarter.

item.title: Cat bond issuance on pace to hit $10bn faster in 2026. But large deals matter for records
item.description: While the first-quarter of 2026 failed to set a new issuance record for the period in the catastrophe bond market, rising activity in recent weeks means that cat bond issuance this year could hit the $10 billion milestone around one week faster than it did a year ago, according to Artemis’ data and analysis. The first-quarter of 2026 saw $6.7 billion of new risk capital coming to market, across full Rule 144A catastrophe bonds and private cat bond deals tracked in the Artemis Deal Directory . catastrophe bond, issuance in 2026 so far has reached almost $7.4 billion.

item.title: Abacab Re cat bond further diversifies Olympus Insurance’s sources of capital: CEO Stroble
item.description: (Series 2026-1) issuance, which provides the firm with fully collateralized, multi-year protection, is set to further diversify Olympus’ sources of capital, while continuing to strengthen its ability to withstand extreme events, CEO Tim Stroble has said. Olympus Insurance Company’s successful sponsorship of its debut catastrophe bond, the $120 million Abacab Re Ltd. He continued: “This transaction provides us with fully collateralized, multi-year protection and further diversifies our sources of capital, while continuing to strengthen our ability to withstand extreme events.

item.title: Spinnaker aims to renew and extend its cat bond coverage, with $100m Mountain Re 2026-1
item.description: The Mountain Re Series 2026-1 Class A cat bond notes will provide Spinnaker with a capital markets backed source of reinsurance against losses from a range of US perils, being US named storm, earthquake, severe thunderstorm, winter storm and fire. As we said, this second Mountain Re cat bond sees Spinnaker looking to expand on its cat bond coverage, with the introduction of the fire peril for the first time, which we understand is largely wildfire risk and located in California. Spinnaker Insurance Company, the personal and commercial lines program and fronting specialist, has returned to the catastrophe bond market for its second sponsorship, seeking $100 million or more in US multi-peril collateralized reinsurance from a Mountain Re Ltd.

item.title: Xceedance hires Swapna Allapur as CPO to drive people strategy for AI-enabled insurance
item.description: Xceedance, a global provider of technology-driven business solutions for the insurance industry, has announced the appointment of Swapna Allapur as Chief People Officer. Allapur’s leadership will be instrumental in ensuring our global teams are further equipped to deliver AI-enabled outcomes with the rigor the industry demands.” Allapur, bringing nearly thirty years of human resources leadership across diverse industries and regions, having spent four years as Chief People Officer at Everest Group, where she focused on integrating talent strategy with key business objectives. In this role, Allapur will lead the company’s global people strategy, focusing on building workforce readiness, enabling large-scale transformation, and strengthening organisational capabilities to help insurers adopt new technologies, including AI, in a responsible and disciplined manner.

item.title: Insurance claims decline further in 2025 as losses grow more complex, Verisk reports
item.description: A report from Verisk, a data analytics and technology provider to the insurance industry, based on data from its ClaimSearch platform, shows that insurance claims continued to fall in 2025, although the risks underpinning those claims became more concentrated and harder to manage, contributing to more severe and prolonged losses. Personal motor claims edged down by nearly 3% in 2025, following a decline of around 5% in the previous year. Claims associated with gig economy activity rose significantly between 2021 and 2025, with gig-related commercial motor claims increasing by 96% and now accounting for 10% of the total.

item.title: Vienna Insurance Group confirms board changes across key subsidiaries
item.description: Vienna Insurance Group, an Austria-based insurance group operating across Central and Eastern Europe, has approved a series of changes to the leadership teams of the Group and its subsidiaries, Wiener Städtische Versicherung and DONAU Versicherung. Following these changes, the Managing Board of Vienna Insurance Group will comprise Hartwig Löger as Chief Executive Officer, Peter Höfinger as First Deputy CEO, Gerhard Lahner as Second Deputy CEO, alongside Judit Havasi, Liane Hirner, Gábor Lehel, Christoph Rath and Sonja Raus. Riener has been a member of the Vienna Insurance Group Managing Board since 2020 and has previously held senior management positions within DONAU.

item.title: Italy Court Allows Class Action Against Meta Over Facebook Data Scraping
item.description: A Milan court on Tuesday accepted a class action brought by a consumer group against Meta Platforms over the theft of personal data suffered by Facebook Italy. According to the court order, the data scraping incident, which took place between January 2018 and September 2019 and was disclosed by Meta in 2021, affected around 533 million Facebook users globally. A legal source estimated that around 35 million Facebook users in Italy could potentially be affected by the data scraping.

item.title: Australian MedTech Firm EBR Systems Flags Possible Data Breach of Patient Records
item.description: Medical technology firm EBR Systems on Wednesday said a cybersecurity incident detected in February may have resulted in unauthorized access to a small amount of personal health information. The company said it became aware of a network disruption around February 13 that affected certain systems, triggering an investigation with the assistance of third‑party computer forensics specialists. The incident underscores Australia’s growing exposure to cybersecurity threats , with the country seeing a sharp rise in data breaches and ransomware attacks over the past five years, including at healthcare, financial and government‑linked organizations.

item.title: AI Ruling Prompts Warnings From Lawyers: Your Chats Could Be Used Against You
item.description: These warnings became more urgent after a federal judge in New York ruled this year that the former CEO of a bankrupt financial services company could not shield his AI chats from prosecutors pursuing securities fraud charges against him. In the wake of the ruling, attorneys have been advising that conversations with chatbots like Anthropic’s Claude and OpenAI’s ChatGPT could be demanded by prosecutors in criminal cases or by litigation adversaries in civil cases. But AI chatbots are not lawyers, and attorneys are instructing clients to take steps that could keep their communications with AI tools more private.

item.title: $3.5bn Aon expands data centre insurance programme capacity amid AI boom
item.description: Aon has increased the capacity of its Data Center Lifecycle Insurance Program (DCLP) to $3.5bn, following a $1bn expansion as demand for data centre risk solutions continues to accelerate. Originally launched in June 2025, the DCLP is a multi-line insurance solution designed to address the interconnected construction, operational, cyber, and financial risks faced by data centre owners, developers, and investors. The move also broadens the programme’s scope to include existing data centres beyond their first year of operations, extending coverage from construction and commissioning into long-term operational phases.

item.title: $2.7bn Standard Life deal for Aegon UK reshapes UK retirement market
item.description: I look forward to welcoming everyone in Aegon UK to Standard Life in due course and working together to capture the huge potential in front of us.” The deal is set to create one of the UK’s largest retirement savings and income providers, reflecting ongoing consolidation in the pensions and life insurance sector as firms scale to meet growing demand for retirement solutions and long-term savings products. Standard Life is the right owner for Aegon UK and a good home for our employees: we share the same values and a strong commitment to customers, and together the businesses will create the UK’s largest retirement savings and income provider. Aegon has agreed to sell its UK insurance arm to Standard Life for $2.7bn, marking the completion of its strategic review of the business.

item.title: Aon expands data center insurance program to $3.5 billion
item.description: Aon expanded its Data Center Lifecycle Insurance Program capacity by $1 billion to $3.5 billion and extended coverage to operational data centers beyond their first year. Launched in 2025, the program targets data center owners, developers, and investors, reflecting growing demand driven by cloud, AI, and hyperscale infrastructure expansion. The program now supports assets across construction and ongoing operations, addressing risks tied to increasingly complex and capital-intensive digital infrastructure.

item.title: Grange Insurance Association to rebrand
item.description: Grange Insurance Association will rebrand as Granwest Insurance effective July 1, 2026, as the Western U.S. “Our transition to Granwest Insurance honors our deep roots while positioning us for the future. insurer looks to modernize its identity while maintaining its focus on families, farms, and businesses across six states; the change will not impact policies, coverage, billing, or agent relationships.

item.title: Aegon to sell Aegon UK to Standard Life for $2.7 billion
item.description: Transamerica owner Aegon announced an agreement to sell it UK insurance arm to Standard Life for $2.7 billion. Standard Life said the deal would create a pensions and savings group with 16 million customers and £480 billion of assets under administration. Standard Life is the right owner for Aegon UK and a good home for our employees: we share the same values and a strong commitment to customers, and together the businesses will create the UK’s largest retirement savings and income provider.

item.title: Grupo Preventiva incorpora nuevo director económico-financiero
item.description: Grupo Preventiva informó ayer de la incorporación de a Javier García Matallanos como nuevo director económico-financiero, cargo desde el que también se integrará en el Comité de Dirección de la compañía. Con más de 20 años de experiencia profesional, ha desarrollado su trayectoria tanto en el ámbito asegurador como en el patrimonial. En el sector seguros, ha ocupado distintas responsabilidades en firmas como Aon, Mutua Madrileña y Filhet-Allard.

item.title: «Las fundaciones corporativas deben ser agentes activos de transformación social»
item.description: Desde el inicio, su visión fue clara: dar forma y continuidad al compromiso social de la entidad, devolviendo a la sociedad, especialmente a los colectivos más vinculados a su actividad, como empleados, agentes, clientes y colaboradores, la confianza recibida a lo largo de su primer siglo de historia. La Fundación MGS conmemora su 20º aniversario consolidada como uno de los principales vehículos de acción social del sector asegurador, tras dos décadas centradas en “mejorar la vida de las personas” y generar impacto social. La Fundación ha cumplido con el propósito para el que nació: canalizar el compromiso social que siempre ha caracterizado a MGS Seguros y contribuir a mejorar la vida de las personas.

item.title: Miura Partners lanza Globaim para impulsar el peritaje y la consultoría de siniestros
item.description: Confrmando la noticia avanzada hoy por el BDS a primera hora de la mañana, Miura Partners acaba de anunciar el lanzamiento de Globaim, una nueva firma especializada en consultoría de siniestros, peritación, ingeniería de riesgos y valoraciones a efectos de seguro . Su plan de crecimiento contempla la adquisición de firmas especializadas, la incorporación de nuevo talento y la apertura de oficinas en estas regiones, con el objetivo de consolidar una plataforma de referencia en consultoría de siniestros y peritaje. Óscar Rodríguez, director general de Globaim, ha señalado que la alianza con Miura Partners permitirá reforzar las capacidades técnicas de la firma y mejorar el servicio ofrecido a clientes y corredores, apoyándose en un modelo basado en la excelencia técnica, la experiencia y el conocimiento de los mercados locales.

item.title: John Hancock Introduces Longevity Preparedness Tool
item.description: John Hancock (Boston) has introduced a personalized longevity preparedness assessment tool developed in collaboration with the MIT AgeLab , designed to help individuals evaluate readiness for longer lifespans across multiple life domains. The tool builds on John Hancock’s and MIT Age Lab’s development of the Longevity Preparedness Index . The release follows earlier research from John Hancock and the MIT AgeLab, including the Longevity Preparedness Index, which established benchmarks for how U.S.

item.title: IBHS Expands Wildfire Prepared Program to 10 More States
item.description: Ten new states are now part of a new wildfire preparedness designation program designed to help homeowners and builders in high-risk areas better protect properties and neighborhoods. The number of states added to the Insurance Institute for Business & Home Safety’s Wildfire Prepared designation is now up to 14. The program offers two levels for individual properties: Wildfire Prepared Home, which outlines actions to reduce risk from wind-driven embers, and Wildfire Prepared Home Plus, an enhanced level of protection against radiant heat and direct flame contact.

item.title: Texas Probes Lululemon for Alleged ‘Forever Chemicals’ Use
item.description: The Texas attorney general’s office is investigating Lululemon Athletica Inc.’s apparel for the presence of so-called “forever chemicals,” which have been linked to health problems. The probe “will examine whether Lululemon’s athletic apparel contains PFAS or ‘forever chemicals’ that their health-conscious customers would not expect based on the brand’s marketing.” Lululemon is cooperating with the Texas attorney general’s inquiry and providing documents, a spokesperson said in an email, adding the company phased out the use of PFAS in fiscal 2023. “Emerging research and consumer concerns have raised questions about the potential presence of certain synthetic materials and chemical compounds in their apparel,” the office of Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton said in a statement.

item.title: Altman-Backed Startup Raises $170 Million for Robocar Networks
item.description: Glydways Inc., the robocar startup backed by Sam Altman, is in talks to raise an additional $250 million on the heels of a roughly $170 million Series C round, underlining the high hopes and daunting hurdles of developing closed-loop self-driving car systems. “We keep getting more customers, which means we need to scale the business faster.” The San Francisco-based company is one of many startups backed by Altman, a prolific investor who led incubator Y Combinator before becoming OpenAI’s CEO. The company, backed also by Khosla Ventures, is seeking a valuation of more than $1 billion for its next round, founder and co-Chief Executive Officer Mark Seeger said in an interview.

item.title: Digbijoy Shukla succeeds Roopa Malhotra as Zurich’s APAC head of digital
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Didier Bélot appointed Marsh Risk Asia’s head of industry
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Sompo APAC appoints Daniel Lukas as SVP, Head of Personal Lines
item.description: Sompo Asia Pacific (Sompo APAC), part of Sompo International Holdings Ltd., has announced the appointment of Daniel Lukas as Senior Vice President, Head of Personal Lines, Asia Pacific, effective 17th April 2026. In a recent LinkedIn post, Sompo APAC said, “With his extensive regional knowledge and technical expertise, Daniel will strengthen the capabilities in Personal Lines underwriting excellence, product innovation and service development, distribution expansion and marketing, to enhance and localise Sompo’s Personal Lines value propositions, while ensuring regional alignment.” Based in Singapore, Lukas will join the APAC Consumer Leadership team and report to Bill Zhang, President of Sompo APAC Consumer Lines.

item.title: The Fidelis Partnership and Pine Walk introduce new London hub at Grace Hall
item.description: The Fidelis Partnership (TFP), a specialty insurance and reinsurance group, together with Pine Walk, its managing general agent (MGA) platform, has opened a new office at Grace Hall in London. Michael Davern, UK CUO & Group Head of D&F Property and Energy of The Fidelis Partnership, said: “Opening this office at the historic Grace Hall is another milestone moment in the outstanding growth of both TFP and the Pine Walk platform. Grace Hall is a Grade II listed building on Leadenhall Street in the City of London, originally established in 1927 as the London base for the family-owned banking firm Grace & Co.

item.title: BHSI promotes Jessica Kirby to Country Manager UK, Nick Major to retire
item.description: Berkshire Hathaway Specialty Insurance (BHSI) has announced the promotion of Jessica Kirby to Country Manager, UK, succeeding Nick Major, who is retiring from the company and the London Market. Nixon joined BHSI in 2019 and was most recently Underwriting Manager, E&P Lines. Kirby, who will be based in London, brings nearly two decades of experience focused on the UK and London markets and deep financial lines expertise.

item.title: With Florida Businesses Facing Higher Costs, is Premium Financing an Answer?
item.description: However, Florida businesses are facing increasing costs of operation, with high insurance premiums dimming some of that sunshine appeal and creating an affordability crisis for some. Timing of Increasing Costs and Cash Flow Considerations Some Florida businesses are deferring expansion, reconsidering hiring practices and limiting their capital expenditures to manage the increasing and unpredictable nature of their insurance costs. Florida’s population is growing rapidly, with over 23 million residents currently, and a projected growth of approximately 1.24% annually.

item.title: CRC Group Adds Murphy as Casualty Broker; Peachtree Group Names Morey VP
item.description: Peachtree Group, an investment management firm based in Atlanta, named Mike Morey to lead the firm’s insurance capital markets strategy. CRC Group, a wholesale specialty insurance distributor, has added a broker to its specialty team, based in Charlotte, North Carolina. Will Murphy has experience in commercial insurance advisory and casualty brokerage, with a focus on business development and client engagement, CRC announced this week.

item.title: Verisk: Insurance Claims Volume Fell to 5-Year Low in 2025
item.description: Commercial auto claims volume fell to 1.8 million in 2025, down 5% from the prior year. Insurance claims volumes in the United States declined across most lines in 2025 due at least in part to less severe weather, but risks remain elevated as insurers navigate complex and concentrated claims, according to an annual report from Verisk. Claims volumes were lower in 2025 than 2024 across personal and commercial lines, Verisk’s Annual Insurance Claims Trends Report found.

item.title: Vertafore Launches Velocity AI Platform Agents
item.description: Vertafore (Denver) has introduced its Velocity AI Platform and a portfolio of six AI agents designed to support insurance workflows for agencies, managing general agents and carriers. And because they’re all built on the same foundation of the Velocity AI platform, they can evolve and connect over time in ways that make the entire system more powerful.” Vertafore says the platform supports its focus on improving distribution processes by reducing operational friction and embedding AI into day-to-day activities across the insurance lifecycle. Vertafore says the platform serves as the foundation for its AI capabilities across its product portfolio, including AgencyOne, MGA solutions and Sircon.

item.title: Tokio Marine HCC Specialty Group Selects Origami Risk Platform
item.description: Tokio Marine HCC-Specialty Group (TMHCC, Houston) has selected Origami Risk (Chicago) to provide its cloud-native property/casualty platform supporting policy, billing and claims operations across its global specialty portfolio. Origami Risk’s platform is purpose‑built to help carriers operate more efficiently, respond quickly to market changes, and deliver outstanding service to partners and policyholders.” Tokio Marine HCC-Specialty Group is part of Tokio Marine Group, which operates globally across specialty insurance markets. Tokio Marine HCC-Specialty Group will implement Origami Risk’s full suite to streamline workflows, improve speed to market and support service delivery across specialty lines.

item.title: ‘Essential to diversify’: Taiping Re looks beyond property cat as market softens, doubles down on Hong Kong, health
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Sompo bolsters Australia underwriting team in major expansion drive
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Rare Earth Insurance revamps leadership across Asia and Europe
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Fire Departments Halt Service Due to Lapse in Workers’ Compensation
item.description: Last week, two volunteer fire departments in Vandergrift Borough, Pennsylvania suspended their services because their workers’ compensation insurance policies lapsed. State law requires fire departments to have the coverage. However, according to Mayor Lenny Collini, the borough arranged for backup from neighboring fire departments for “uninterrupted emergency response coverage” until the insurance issue is fixed.

item.title: Vertafore launches ReferenceConnect AI, powered by Velocity AI, to transform how insurance professionals access trusted knowledge
item.description: The post Vertafore launches ReferenceConnect AI, powered by Velocity AI, to transform how insurance professionals access trusted knowledge appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights . New AI-powered experience delivers up to 80% faster answers, combining verified insurance content with the speed and ease of natural language search Vertafore announced the launch of ReferenceConnect AI , an AI-powered experience built on the Vertafore Velocity AI Platform , to transform how insurance professionals access and apply trusted industry knowledge in their everyday work. That’s how we’re applying AI to remove friction and help the industry move toward greater Distribution Velocity.” ReferenceConnect AI reflects Vertafore’s strategy to embed AI directly into the workflows where insurance work happens—turning trusted knowledge into faster decisions.

item.title: Sedgwick takes full ownership of Dutch injury assistance JV
item.description: Sedgwick has acquired the remaining minority stake in Dé Letselschade Hulpdienst (LSHD), taking full ownership of the Netherlands-based personal injury assistance provider after more than a decade as a joint venture. The stake was purchased from Raasveld Holding, giving Sedgwick full control of a business that supports injured individuals immediately following an accident. Sedgwick said the move strengthens its position in the Netherlands and allows it to further address gaps in the early stages of the claims and recovery process, where coordination and timely support can influence outcomes.

item.title: Albertsons Reaches $774 Million Opioid Accord
item.description: announced a $774 million settlement to resolve all opioid-related claims, making the grocery chain one of the last major retailers to reach such a deal. Albertsons is the latest big retailer to reach a settlement in the wake of the opioid crisis. Due to the settlement, the company recorded a charge of about $600 million last quarter, net of tax, that left it reporting a loss of roughly $480 million.

item.title: Millions of Rockstar Games Business Records Stolen, Hackers Say
item.description: A hacking group with a history of compromising major global corporations said it had stolen nearly 80 million business records from video game developer Rockstar Games, the maker of Grand Theft Auto. A representative of the hacking group told Reuters in an online chat on Monday it has 78.6 million records from Rockstar’s account with Snowflake, a large corporate data management company. Rockstar’s Snowflake data was accessed via a compromise of data from Anodot, an AI-powered business analytics platform, the hacking group representative said.

item.title: Verisk: Claims Volumes Fell to Five-Year Low in 2025 as Emerging Risks Intensify
item.description: Insurance claims volumes in the United States declined across most lines in 2025, but risks remain elevated as insurers navigate complex and concentrated claims, according to an annual report from Verisk. Claims volumes were lower in 2025 than 2024 across personal and commercial lines, Verisk’s Annual Insurance Claims Trends Report found. There were 5.3 million homeowners claims in 2025, the lowest level in the past five years and down 19% year-over-year from 6.5 million in 2024.

item.title: IBHS Wildfire Prepared Program Expands to 10 More States
item.description: Expanding our Wildfire Prepared designation program allows more families and neighbors to take coordinated, science-based action to reduce risk and strengthen resilience.” Established in 2022, Wildfire Prepared is built on the latest IBHS research examining how homes ignite during wildfire events and what mitigation measures most effectively interrupt structure-to-structure spread. Fourteen – that’s the total number of states that will now have access to the Insurance Institute for Business & Home Safety’s Wildfire Prepared designation program. The research-based wildfire mitigation program brings science-based wildfire mitigation and third-party verification to more property owners in high-risk regions.

item.title: Reinsurers Stand Apart as M&A Buyers; Many Re Deals Destroy Value: Analysis
item.description: The report, “Carrier Mergers & Acquisitions: Drivers, Implications & Outcomes,” published by the insurance industry standards-setting body ACORD , notes that publicly traded reinsurers and multilines (writing both property/casualty and life/health insurance) buyers in M&A deals stand apart from P/C and L/H carriers, who are largely value creators. A recently released analysis of insurance carrier and reinsurer mergers and acquisitions finds that 75% of recent deals featuring reinsurers and multiline insurers as buyers destroyed value for shareholders. 31, 2025, and indexed against the MSCI World Index to isolate deal-specific outcomes from broader equity market movements.) Related article : Nearly Half of 100 Largest P/C Insurers Destroy Value: ACORD As a group, reinsurers and multiline carriers produced 13.3% lower returns than the market between close date and year-end 2025, while post-deal returns for P/C insurers were 3.1% above the overall equity market.

item.title: Despite Reduced Claims Volume, Complex Risks Grow: Report
item.description: Though insurance claims declined in 2025, underlying risks became more complex and concentrated, leading to more severe and challenging losses for many insurers, according to its annual ClaimsSearch Trends Report. Lower claims volume in 2025 masks shifting risks from emerging trends, wildfires, and targeted auto theft, according to Verisk, a data analytics and tech provider to the global insurance market. Commercial auto claims volume fell 5 percent in 2025 but remained 14 percent higher than in 2021, reflecting longer-term expansion of commercial driving risk, the report noted.

item.title: Collateralised fronting the centrepiece of Hannover Re’s broad ILS offering: Sehm & Horstmann
item.description: While Hannover Re has expanded its range of insurance-linked securities services and facilitation, responding to market demand, the company sees collateralised fronting as the centrepiece of this capital market focused aspect of its global reinsurance operations, executives Silke Sehm and Patrick Horstmann told us in an interview. Now that new division is operational, we spoke with Hannover Re Executive Board Member Silke Sehm and Head of ILS Patrick Horstmann, who stressed that the reinsurer wants to be a dependable and scalable partner for the ILS market and its constituents, across all the different offerings. “Our role is to act as a facilitator, offering a dependable and scalable set of solutions that work for both cedants and investors across market cycles.” Moving on to discuss the recent Bermuda unit launch, Sehm explained how it rounds out Hannover Re’s offering in ILS, “With the successful launch of our Bermuda-based sidecar structure, Hannover Re Capital Partners, HCP, we now provide services for the full spectrum of ILS instruments.
